name: Cool Dock
goal: A native macOS app (shipping as Dock+) that adds a floating second Dock of live widgets beside the system Dock.
done: Built a floating glass NSPanel second-Dock above the system Dock, all-Spaces and edge-selectable
Shipped v0.2 with 14 widgets (Clock, Weather, Music, Calendar, Email, System, Battery, App launcher, Folders, Bookmarks, Emoji, Ask Claude, 1Password, Quick actions)
Made widgets interactive/expandable (Music player popover, Calendar urgency color-coding, in-tile typing)
Added Dock auto-hide awareness (lifts when the hidden Dock reveals), discreet minimize, and a Settings permissions guide
Added drag-to-reorder and a curated 10-widget default in Settings
Renamed Cool Dock to Dock+ (display name; bundle id and Xcode target kept stable)
Built a static docs site (features/widgets/instructions/FAQ/privacy) for brito.ai/apps/dock-plus
Verified dark mode, SwiftLint 0 violations, BUILD SUCCEEDED, and ran code-review + doc-review passes

Fixed a launch crash (Dock+ died ~0.3s after opening): the Reminders EventKit completion closure inherited @MainActor isolation but ran on a background queue, tripping Swift 6's executor assertion; marked it @Sendable, rebuilt, reinstalled over /Applications/Dock+.app, verified it stays alive through the reminders fetch
Added launch-at-login via SMAppService (auto-enables first run, self-heals the registered path to /Applications, Settings toggle); verified enabled via sfltool dumpbtm
New Dia Tabs widget: reads the Dia browser's open tabs over AppleScript and jumps to one on click (verified live reading 56 tabs); confirmed Warp exposes no scripting API so its tabs can't be read

Polish round: gave each widget tile its own frosted VisualEffectView backing so tiles read as solid cards against the 60%-transparent bar frame (fill wash 0.16/0.24), fixed the clock widget wrapping the AM/PM onto a second line, folders double-click-to-open-in-Finder + taller contents popover, tightened the gap to the system Dock, and a Reminders list picker (Today/All/specific list, default Today)
Initialized git and pushed the project to a private GitHub repo (juniorbrito0) as the first commit

Ran a 14-agent review workflow (subsystem finders + adversarial verify); fixed all 9 confirmed medium bugs (osascript watchdog timeout, off-main EventKit/FS work, resilient widget persistence, safe log writes, TCC-hang activation-policy restore) plus cleanups (coalesced refreshes, shared tile chrome, once-a-minute clock, brand signature)
Open-sourced the project: added README + MIT LICENSE, made the GitHub repo public, cut Release v0.1.0
Packaged a Developer ID-signed, Apple-notarized + stapled Dock-Plus.dmg (Gatekeeper: Notarized Developer ID) and attached it to the GitHub release (stable /releases/latest/download link)

Took the Dock+ site live: removed the 'in the works' gate from brito.ai/apps/dock-plus, wired the DMG download + GitHub links, updated docs to 15 widgets incl. Dia Tabs + launch-at-login, deployed to Cloudflare Pages and verified (daylura unaffected)
in_progress: Widget breadth toward Cooldock parity (~40 target)
True 'beside the Dock' auto-positioning, per-widget config, multi-display handling

development.notes: Active build; 14 widgets working, SwiftLint 0 and BUILD SUCCEEDED verified.

features.notes: Core widgets shipped; roadmap targets more widgets, reorder polish, launch-at-login.

uiux.notes: Design system (graphite glass + electric-teal accent) built; dark mode verified; app icon still a placeholder.

branding.notes: Named Dock+; distinctive glass identity in place; distinctive app icon not yet chosen.

marketing.notes: Static docs site built under site/ for brito.ai/apps/dock-plus.

sales.notes: Freemium/IAP planned per house style but not yet built; app not submitted to the App Store.

next_steps: Design and pick a distinctive app icon to replace the current placeholder.
Implement true 'beside the Dock' auto-positioning by detecting the system Dock's size and edge.
Add a first-run onboarding flow for the OS permission prompts (Automation, Location, Calendar, Reminders, Accessibility).
Localize the UI to EN + PT-BR per house style before any public release.
Decide the distribution path (App Store submission with freemium/IAP, or direct download).
insights: Much of the app's value is gated behind first-run OS permissions (Automation, Location, Calendar, Accessibility); onboarding that walks users through these grants is essential or the widgets look broken.
14 widgets already overflow one screen — widget breadth toward ~40 parity should be balanced against a tight default set so the dock stays usable.
Heavy reliance on AppleScript bridges (Music, Mail) is fragile across macOS versions; those integrations need re-verification each OS update.
The app is unsigned/unsubmitted with a placeholder icon — the remaining distance to a shippable product is packaging and distribution, not core features.

Swift 6 strict concurrency turns actor-isolation mistakes into hard launch crashes, not warnings: any framework callback that fires on a background queue (EventKit, and similarly the AppleScript/NSWorkspace bridges) must be @Sendable/nonisolated. Audit the other services for the same pattern.

milestones.title: v0.1 scaffold + working floating second dock
Rename to Dock+ and docs site
